narrative:
The Karnataka High Court heard 713 of 2,680 scheduled matters on 2026-04-09, an overall hearing rate of 26.6%. That is lower than the retrieved 2026-04-02 context, when 774 of 2,403 matters were heard for a 32.2% rate. The court therefore listed more matters today but converted a smaller share of the board into actual hearings.

Judge-wise, the strongest conversion on meaningful dockets came from `K.S. Hemalekha` at 48 of 67 matters heard (71.6%), `R. Nataraj` at 44 of 72 (61.1%), and `Pradeep Singh Yerur` at 38 of 65 (58.5%). `Mohammad Nawaz & Venkatesh Naik T` posted the highest rate among mid-sized boards at 29 of 36 (80.6%). By contrast, some large boards moved slowly: `V Srishananda` heard 6 of 89 (6.7%), while one `S.R. Krishna Kumar` board heard only 4 of 184 matters (2.2%). The `Chief Justice & C.M. Poonacha` benches together heard 14 of 120 listed matters, about 11.7%.

Stage conversion was led by `UNKNOWN` matters at 35.7%, followed by `PRELIMINARY HEARING` at 27.7%, `ADMISSION` at 26.9%, and `ORDERS` at 25.7%. `FINAL HEARING` was the weakest major stage at 19.9%, though that is still above the 16.3% seen on 2026-04-02. The sharper historical drop is in `ADMISSION`, down from 49.2% on 2026-04-02 to 26.9% today.

Case-type outcomes were uneven. `WP` dominated the board with 1,205 listings and 304 hearings, but its 25.2% clearance was below the overall average and below 28.9% on 2026-04-02. Better-moving categories included `RSA` at 48.6%, `MFA` at 41.2%, `CRL.A` at 41.1%, and `RFA` at 35.2%, while `CRL.P` (15.8%), `CCC` (5.2%), `CRL.RP` (7.1%), `CMP` (0%), and `RERA.A` (0%) saw weak disposal.
